title_en: Zakat and Fasting in Kitab al-Umm
content_en:
The zakat and fasting chapters of Kitab al-Umm present ash-Shafi'i's original arguments for the Shafi'i school's positions on these two pillars, including his direct engagements with the positions of Abu Hanifah and Malik ibn Anas. These chapters are particularly valuable for understanding the formative period of Shafi'i jurisprudence and for seeing how the great imam himself reasoned about the sources.

Ash-Shafi'i's treatment of zakat begins with a thorough examination of the Quranic commands and the prophetic hadiths establishing each category of zakatable property. His approach — beginning with the explicit Quranic text, then adding the prophetic hadith that specifies details, then addressing questions not resolved by either — models the legal methodology he elaborated in the Risalah.

On the zakat of minor children's wealth, ash-Shafi'i argues in Kitab al-Umm for the Shafi'i position (that the guardian pays zakat from the minor's wealth) by citing the hadith 'Trade with orphans' wealth lest zakat consume it' (al-Bayhaqi) and the reasoning that the Quranic zakat commands are not restricted to adults. He directly engages with the Hanafi position (no zakat on minors) and finds it unsupported by explicit hadith evidence.

Ash-Shafi'i's treatment of agricultural zakat establishes the Shafi'i restriction to crops that can be dried and stored. He explains the reasoning: the nisab of five awsuq was specified as a weight/volume measure, which presupposes a commodity that can be measured in dried form. Perishable produce cannot be measured in the standardized form the prophetic texts envision, and therefore the specific obligation does not extend to it. This is an example of ash-Shafi'i's careful reasoning from the form of the prophetic text rather than merely from its apparent subject matter.

The fasting chapter in Kitab al-Umm covers the Ramadan fast with ash-Shafi'i's characteristic attention to the hadith evidence. His discussion of the nighttime intention requirement is explicit: the Shafi'i school requires the intention to be made each night for each day, based on the hadith 'Whoever does not have the intention before dawn, there is no fast for them' (Ahmad, Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi — with some discussion of its exact chain). This is the primary Shafi'i argument against the Maliki allowance of a single intention for the entire month.

On what breaks the fast, ash-Shafi'i's Kitab al-Umm contains an important discussion of the concept of 'what reaches the body cavity' (wusul ila al-jawf). His analysis is more restrictive than the Hanafi approach: he holds that any substance that reaches the body cavity through any open channel breaks the fast if done intentionally, while the Hanafi school focuses primarily on the three explicitly mentioned acts (eating, drinking, sexual intercourse) and treats other cases more narrowly.

Ash-Shafi'i's discussion of the kaffarah for deliberately breaking the Ramadan fast establishes the Shafi'i position that the kaffarah is triggered by sexual intercourse during the fast day, not by other acts of deliberate fast-breaking. Other acts require qada only, not kaffarah, in the Shafi'i school. His argument is based on the specific wording of the prophetic hadith establishing the kaffarah — which refers to a specific act of intercourse — and his reluctance to extend obligations beyond what the text explicitly establishes.
title_id: Zakat dan Puasa dalam Kitab al-Umm
content_id:
Bab-bab zakat dan puasa dari Kitab al-Umm menyajikan argumen-argumen asli asy-Syafi'i untuk posisi-posisi madzhab Syafi'i tentang dua pilar ini, termasuk keterlibatan langsungnya dengan posisi-posisi madzhab Hanafi dan Maliki. Argumen-argumen asy-Syafi'i untuk pendekatan Syafi'i terhadap zakat — kategori-kategorinya, laju-lajunya, dan kondisi-kondisi yang mengatur kewajiban — disajikan dengan metodologi argumen dari teks-teks hadis yang beliau anggap paling otoritatif yang menjadi ciri khas karya ini.

Diskusi tentang nisab zakat sangat berguna: asy-Syafi'i mempresentasikan teks-teks hadis yang menurutnya menetapkan nisab-nisab untuk berbagai kategori kekayaan, merespons interpretasi-interpretasi alternatif dari para ahli hukum lain, dan mengartikulasikan alasan-alasannya untuk memilih interpretasi Syafi'i. Proses argumen ini jarang terlihat dalam teks-teks Syafi'i belakangan yang cukup menyatakan posisi madzhab tanpa mendokumentasikan proses yang menghasilkannya.

Bab-bab puasa dalam Kitab al-Umm sangat kaya dalam diskusi tentang kondisi-kondisi yang membatalkan puasa dan konsekuensi-konsekuensi dari berbagai jenis pembatalan. Pendekatan asy-Syafi'i terhadap pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini — memperhatikan penentuan niat puasa, kondisi-kondisi di mana kafarat diperlukan, dan status puasa qadha — menetapkan fondasi bagi perlakuan-perlakuan Syafi'i belakangan dari topik-topik ini dan menampilkan metodologi yang menggabungkan analisis teks yang ketat dengan perhatian terhadap kondisi-kondisi praktis kehidupan Muslim.
